channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 5th member of Iranian women's soccer team gives up asylum in Australia
item.description: The player's departure shortly before midnight on Sunday leaves two of an initial seven squad members in Australia.
item.content:encoded:
The player's departure shortly before midnight on Sunday leaves two of an initial seven squad members in Australia.

(Image credit: STR/AFP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: An Iranian-American writer describes the "vertigo" he feels about the war
item.description: Host Adrian Ma speaks with Iranian American writer Nick Mafi about the war in Iran. Mafi says many Iranians in the US are feeling a sense of vertigo because of the conflict.
item.content:encoded: Host Adrian Ma speaks with Iranian American writer Nick Mafi about the war in Iran. Mafi says many Iranians in the US are feeling a sense of vertigo because of the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: In Indonesia, a fishing village replants mangrove forests one seedling at a time
item.description: Over the last 30 years, 40% of Indonesia's mangroves have been cut down mainly to create fish and shrimp farms, destroying local ecosystems. But one small indigenous fishing village is changing that.
item.content:encoded: Over the last 30 years, 40% of Indonesia's mangroves have been cut down mainly to create fish and shrimp farms, destroying local ecosystems. But one small indigenous fishing village is changing that.
item.dc:creator: Katerina Barton

item.title: Israeli soldiers fire on family car in occupied West Bank, killing 4
item.description: Israeli soldiers fired on a car carrying a family in the northern West Bank, killing four people including two children, the Palestinian Authority's Health Ministry said.
item.content:encoded:
Israeli soldiers fired on a car carrying a family in the northern West Bank, killing four people including two children, the Palestinian Authority's Health Ministry said.

(Image credit: Majdi Mohammed)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: 'We never asked for a ceasefire,' says Iran's foreign minister, as war keeps raging
item.description: Israel announced a barrage of new strikes on western Iran on Sunday, while Iran's foreign minister said the country has not asked for a ceasefire as President Trump had claimed.
item.content:encoded:
Israel announced a barrage of new strikes on western Iran on Sunday, while Iran's foreign minister said the country has not asked for a ceasefire as President Trump had claimed.

(Image credit: Ohad Zwigenberg)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: In a small Lebanese town, grief and fear follow the Michigan synagogue attack
item.description: The suspect who attacked a synagogue in Michigan lost family members in an Israeli strike in Lebanon on March 5. Relatives and neighbors in his hometown share their views on his actions.
item.content:encoded:
The suspect who attacked a synagogue in Michigan lost family members in an Israeli strike in Lebanon on March 5. Relatives and neighbors in his hometown share their views on his actions.

(Image credit: JOSEPH EID)
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi

item.title: Russian strike on Kyiv region kills 4 and wounds 15, with peace talks stalled
item.description: The strikes comes after the United States paused ceasefire talks between Russia and Ukraine due to the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded:
The strikes comes after the United States paused ceasefire talks between Russia and Ukraine due to the war with Iran.

(Image credit: Efrem Lukatsky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Brazil's ex-President Bolsonaro is in intensive care with pneumonia, hospital says
item.description: One of Bolsonaro's doctors described the former Brazilian president's medical condition as "serious."
item.content:encoded:
One of Bolsonaro's doctors described the former Brazilian president's medical condition as "serious."

(Image credit: Eraldo Peres)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How the conflict in Iran is affecting global markets
item.description: Escalation of the Iran conflict is sending shockwaves through global markets — driving up oil, fuel, and commodity prices, stoking inflation and recession risks worldwide. We hear from three NPR reporters in Europe, Asia and Russia.
item.content:encoded: Escalation of the Iran conflict is sending shockwaves through global markets — driving up oil, fuel, and commodity prices, stoking inflation and recession risks worldwide. We hear from three NPR reporters in Europe, Asia and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: U.S. Embassy in Baghdad again urges Americans to leave Iraq as Trump touts strikes on Iran
item.description: In a post on Truth Social, President Trump claimed the U.S. had "destroyed 100% of Iran's Military capability." The U.S. Embassy in Baghdad, meanwhile, urged Americans to "leave Iraq immediately."
item.content:encoded:
In a post on Truth Social, President Trump claimed the U.S. had "destroyed 100% of Iran's Military capability." The U.S. Embassy in Baghdad, meanwhile, urged Americans to "leave Iraq immediately."

(Image credit: Jalaa Marey)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff
